chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 1578 of 2021 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case (priority) 1. For orders on office objection at Flag-A 2. For hearing of CMA No.7625/2021 (S/A) 3. For hearing of main case 17-11-2021 Mr. Saeed Jamal Lund, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. Ali Raza Baloch, Assistant Advocate General Sindh along with Dr. Muhammad Hanif Bhanwar, Additional Medical Superintendent, PMC Hospital, Shaheed Benazirabad and Dr. Ghulam Rasool, Additional Medical Superintendent, KMC Hospital, Khairpur..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Through this Petition, the Petitioner has impugned Notification dated 29-10-2021, through which his earlier transfer vide Notification dated 23-02-2021 from Peoples Medical College Hospital, Nawabshah / Shaheed Benazirabad to Khairpur Medical College, Civil Hospital, Khairpur has been cancelled / withdrawn. Counsel for the Petitioner submits that the Petitioner cannot be transferred back to Nawabshah / Shaheed Benazirabad as he is entitled for the benefit of Wedlock Policy. Notice was ordered and comments have been filed today, wherein an objection has been raised by the learned AAG that insofar as the Petitioner’s claim under the Wedlock Policy is concerned, he has never approached the Department for such purpose, whereas, the application, purportedly filed with the Secretary, Health Department, was never received as there is no acknowledgement on record. In view of such position, without touching merits of the case, Petition is disposed of with pending application by directing the Secretary, Health Department, Government of Sindh, Karachi to treat this Petition and its annexures as an application for claim of the Petitioner under the Wedlock Policy as may be prevailing and such application be decided within a period of thirty days from today in accordance with law.

Notice was ordered and comments have been filed today, wherein an objection has been raised by the learned AAG that insofar as the Petitioner’s claim under the Wedlock Policy is concerned, he has never approached the Department for such purpose, whereas, the application, purportedly filed with the Secretary, Health Department, was never received as there is no acknowledgement on record. In view of such position, without touching merits of the case, Petition is disposed of with pending application by directing the Secretary, Health Department, Government of Sindh, Karachi to treat this Petition and its annexures as an application for claim of the Petitioner under the Wedlock Policy as may be prevailing and such application be decided within a period of thirty days from today in accordance with law. Let copy of this order be issued to AAG and Secretary Health, Government of Sindh. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
